proof_package.semantics: artifact bundle completeness keyed by parts.*

corpus_scope: source, schema, example, expected-failure, test, evaluation, and promotion FRE surfaces
goal-versus-implementation comparison across the governed proof package
per-phase comparison evidence challenge for verification, health, and promotion claims

lifecycle_phases.objective: Identify the exact comparison-bearing FRE surfaces and insertion points that must reference InfraNodus.
Scope InfraNodus rows to comparison core, supporting skills, and explicit non-scope without inventing new rows.
Place InfraNodus dependencies coherently across source, schema, examples, expected failures, tests, evaluation, and promotion.
Enforce InfraNodus hook fields only where comparison-bearing artifacts require them.
Make validators and expected-failure fixtures fail when required InfraNodus hooks are missing.
Run the bounded Draft 2020-12 schema, example, pytest, and diff checks without claiming broader substrate truth.
Keep promotion distinct from evaluation and require comparison mapping plus evidence in the promotion records.

proof_package_phases.objective: Validate and update source surfaces, including provenance, prompt-contract traceability, and InfraNodus scope selection.
Validate governed schemas and keep InfraNodus schema hooks current for the comparison-bearing surfaces.
Validate fixtures and preserve invalid examples for missing comparison fields.
Keep the expected-failures registry aligned to exact missing-comparison-hook reasons.
Run contract tests and enforce InfraNodus hook requirements explicitly.
Refresh evaluation artifacts, checks, and InfraNodus-backed comparison evidence without collapsing promotion into evaluation.
Refresh promotion artifacts from exact evaluation outputs and keep promotion as a distinct evidence layer.

mode: Treat this as one continuous autonomous run.
Do not proceed to the next phase until the InfraNodus step for the current phase is complete and its artifacts are written.
mission: Execute the seven proof-package phases in order while computing lifecycle gate governance in parallel, and make the associated InfraNodus comparison action the last step of each phase.
current_verified_state: The governed prompt schema set lives under meta/harness/docs/specs and meta/harness/docs/copilot-prompting-playbook.md.
The active FRE lane lives under meta/harness/fre on the nested VW_iTwin_Bridge/VW_iTwin_Bridge surface.
The InfraNodus authority set lives under analysis/infranodus and analysis/capabilities.
hard_rules: No fake green. Fail closed on drift.
Do not collapse evaluation or promotion into tests.
Keep evaluation and promotion as explicit artifact layers under meta/harness/fre/evaluation and meta/harness/fre/promotion.
Treat the lifecycle gates schema and proof package schema as distinct authorities and do not merge them.
allowed_paths: Patch only the required FRE lane and InfraNodus authority artifacts for this seven-phase bounded run.
disallowed_paths: Do not merge the authoritative contracts.
tasks: Verify source surfaces and fix prompt trace alignment first if needed.
For each proof-package phase, also compute the impacted lifecycle gate progress keyed by gate_id.
Run schema, example, and contract validations in sequence and stop on the first real failure.
Refresh expected-failure, evaluation, and promotion artifacts only from exact live outputs.
Perform the required InfraNodus artifact update as the final step of each phase.
validation_loop: Run the named validation command for the current phase and capture its output in the governed artifact path.
Stop immediately on failure and propose the smallest bounded fix.
comparison: InfraNodus is mandatory and authoritative for the comparison-bearing surfaces in every phase.
configuration: The library spine is mandatory for capability, prompt, reference, and job bindings.
Pixeltable substrate refs must be carried wherever a bounded artifact claims durable execution or promotion truth.
success_criteria: Each phase completes with its own InfraNodus artifact update written last.
Evaluation and promotion stay explicit, separate, and evidence-backed.
The final report can cite exact per-phase artifact updates and bounded validation outputs.
report_contract: Written artifacts under meta/harness/fre/evaluation and meta/harness/fre/promotion.
git status --short for meta/harness/fre and analysis/infranodus and analysis/capabilities.
